name: Sesame Street
description: Big Bird, Elmo, Oscar, Cookie Monster — the longest-running children's show on TV.

questions.q: What color is Elmo's fur?
Which Muppet lives in a trash can on Sesame Street?
What color is Oscar the Grouch?

source.notes: Oscar was actually orange in Sesame Street's first season; switched to green in Season 2.

questions.q: What is the name of Big Bird's teddy bear?
A persistent urban legend (denied by Sesame Workshop) claims Bert and Ernie were named after which two characters from 'It's a Wonderful Life'?
choices: The angel Clarence and Uncle Billy
Bert the cop and Ernie the cab driver

source.original_a: Bert the cop and Ernie the cab driver
source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: framed as urban legend explicitly. Head writer Jon Stone said the names came from looking at the prototype puppets; Sesame Workshop and Jerry Juhl have denied the It's a Wonderful Life connection, though the matching names persist as folklore.

questions.q: Which Sesame Street character is famous for his love of cookies?
What color is Grover?
What is the name of Oscar the Grouch's pet worm?
What is the name of Bert's pet pigeon?
What is the name of Elmo's pet goldfish?
In what year did Sesame Street first air?
Who co-created Sesame Street?
choices: Joan Ganz Cooney and Lloyd Morrisett

source.original_a: Joan Ganz Cooney and Lloyd Morrisett
source.notes: Cooney was a TV producer; Morrisett was a Carnegie Corp. executive who co-founded the Children's Television Workshop.

questions.q: Who designed and originally puppeteered the Muppets?
Which puppeteer played Big Bird (and Oscar the Grouch) for nearly 50 years?

source.original_a: Caroll Spinney until 2018, then Matt Vogel
source.notes: Spinney played both characters from 1969 to 2018.

questions.q: What is Big Bird's best friend's full name?

source.notes: Aloysius Snuffleupagus, called 'Snuffy' for short. A woolly-mammoth-like creature.

questions.q: What instrument does Hoots the Owl play?
Which Muppet narrates 'The Monster at the End of This Book'?

source.notes: Loveable, furry old Grover begs the reader not to turn the page — only to find himself at the end.

questions.q: What is Ernie's most beloved bath-time song?

source.notes: Released 1970; reached #16 on the Billboard Hot 100.

questions.q: Which two languages does Rosita speak on Sesame Street?

source.notes: Full name: Rosita, La Monstrua de las Cuevas.

questions.q: Which character is famous for counting everything in sight, often punctuated with thunderclaps?

source.notes: A vampire-themed Muppet whose voice and laugh ('Ah-ah-ah!') parody Bela Lugosi's Dracula.
